summary:
Topical steroids are most effective in eczematous dermatitis because they reduce inflammation and relieve symptoms through their immunosuppressive mechanisms. They are ineffectual and may be harmful in viral skin infections like herpes simplex virus-induced eczema herpeticum or shingles, which require specific antiviral therapies instead.  

\boxed{B}
